pe_discount.detail: Fwd PE 8.99 vs implied PE 13.62 (target_median 83.50 / fwd_eps 6.131) = 34.0% discount.

dividend.detail: Yield 2.4% with payout ratio 27.2% — well covered.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership reported 105.2% (data quirk, effectively saturated); recommendation_mean 2.18 with 13/28 buy-or-better.

ai_exposure.detail: IT services/outsourcing model is exposed to GenAI productivity displacement of routine app dev and BPO work, though Cognizant is repositioning around AI services per company description.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 9.11% with 4.9% MoM build is notable but not extreme; 5.76 days-to-cover offers modest squeeze potential on any positive surprise.

catalysts_general: Trades at 34% discount to implied PE from analyst median target
Forward PE 8.99 with PEG 0.96 implies cheap relative to growth
Earnings growth 22.1% YoY despite revenue growth only 4.9%
Sitting roughly at 52w low ($54.48) — tactical support level
Dividend yield 2.4% with low 27% payout ratio leaves capital return room

downside_scenarios.scenario: Forward PE re-rates to 8.0 on outsourcing demand fears
Recession + AI-displacement narrative compresses multiple to 7
Earnings cut 15% and multiple compresses to 6 in deep cycle

ideal_entry.reasoning: Modest break of 52w low offers asymmetric setup with FCF yield ~7.6% and ~36% upside to median target.

comparables.note: Direct peer, larger and richer multiple, also drawn down 45%
Direct peer, similar multiple but faster growth
Indian IT peer; not in input — referenced for context

value_picks.analyst_take: Cleanest setup of the group: 6/6 framework score with double-digit drawdown, 7%+ FCF yield, net cash, dividend, and 34% discount to consensus. Primary risk is GenAI commoditization of services revenue, but at 9x forward earnings the market is already pricing meaningful disruption.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 84.7% > revenue growth 65.9% — operating leverage intact.

capital_efficiency.detail: Return on equity 212.9% (small book base inflates ratio, but capital productivity is exceptional).

deal_radar.assessment: Trading 39% below 52w high while fundamentals remain elite; 20d momentum (+16.1%) suggests base-building after sharp drawdown. Mean analyst target $639 implies 41% upside.

26 of 30 analysts at Buy/Strong Buy; recommendation mean 1.47.

Growth deceleration concerns + reset toward 52w-low support zone.

growth_picks.name: Palantir Technologies Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 70.0% — accelerating commercial AIP adoption.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 647.6% >> revenue growth 70% — extreme operating leverage as fixed costs leverage AIP scale.

deal_radar.assessment: Framework perfect, but valuation is the dominant risk: P/S 75.7x and forward P/E 76x leave no margin for execution slip. 32% drawdown is healthy reset but mean target $186 implies only 32% upside despite ideal fundamentals.

growth_picks.name: NVIDIA Corporation

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 73.2% on $216B base — extraordinary at scale.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 95.6% > revenue growth 73.2% — continued operating leverage on AI demand.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 1.21% (well below 10%) but rising +13.1% MoM — borderline; low absolute SI mitigates the rising trend.

ai_exposure.detail: NVDA is the foundational compute platform for AI; data-center revenue continues to compound on hyperscaler and sovereign AI buildouts.

deal_radar.assessment: Setup is strongest of the AI complex: only 5.9% from 52w high, 20d return +16.6%, PEG 0.72 (cheap on growth-adjusted basis). Mean target $268.61 implies 35% upside on largest-cap stock in market.

catalysts_general: Trading 26% below mean analyst target ($268.61).
Forward P/E 17.8 vs trailing 40.8 — significant earnings expansion priced in.
PEG 0.72 — cheapest large-cap growth at this scale.
56 of 59 analysts at Buy/Strong Buy; recommendation mean 1.29 — strongest of the group.
Free cash flow $58.1B; net cash $51B.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Modest pullback on hyperscaler capex pacing concerns.
AI-cycle digestion or competitive pressure from custom silicon (TPU/ASIC).

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near 20-day SMA ($186.53); accumulation zone with thesis intact and ~7% better entry.

comparables.note: Slower-growing AI peer at higher fwd P/E — NVDA is paradoxically cheaper.

growth_picks.analyst_take: The most asymmetric large-cap setup in tech: 73% revenue growth on a $216B base at a 17.8x forward P/E (PEG 0.72). With 56 of 59 analysts at Buy and the stock only 6% from highs, the consensus 35% upside reflects continued earnings beats rather than multiple expansion.
